task.title: Decide command-head compaction boundaries for uneven multiline call sites
task.summary: Some multiline calls now compact only the head and first one or two arguments, producing shapes that are acceptable but still feel uneven.
task.problem: The review marked the `find-highest-version-dir ...` call-site compactions in configure-apps.nu as mixed-but-acceptable rather than clearly good or clearly bad. We should decide whether that is the intended house style or a transitional artifact of the current command-head rejoining heuristic.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Choose whether partially compacted multiline call sites like the find-highest-version-dir cases are desirable.
Encode the chosen policy with regression tests using the real configure-apps.nu call shapes.
Document the intended behavior so future diffs can be judged against an explicit rule instead of taste.
